title: DeChambeau and Rahm: Twin Titans Head to Augusta in White-Hot Form
excerpt: Two-time majors winners Bryson DeChambeau and Jon Rahm arrive at the Masters with combined recent form that has them as the players everyone else is chasing. A showdown looms.
body:
The Masters Reckoning

In 72 hours, Bryson DeChambeau and Jon Rahm will walk onto Augusta National as golf's hottest men. The two-time major winners have dominated LIV Golf's opening stretch, combining for three victories in the circuit's first five events and leaving the rest of the field — and the wider golfing world — gasping in their wake. With the Masters just five days away, the stage is perfectly set for a reckoning.

DeChambeau Form
1st, 1st
Rahm Form
2nd, 1st, 2nd
Rahm Points Lead
300+
LIV Players at Augusta
10

The Form Book Tells the Story

DeChambeau's last two weeks have been nothing short of extraordinary. He defeated Richard T. Lee in a playoff at Singapore, then came back days later to edge Rahm — himself in imperious form — on the first playoff hole at South Africa. That’s back-to-back majors-calibre performances on the biggest stages outside the established tour structure. Meanwhile, Rahm has posted a 2-1-2 record through five events, maintaining the consistency that saw him win LIV Golf Hong Kong by three strokes and finish second three times. He leads the season-long points race by over 300 points — a commanding margin.

When asked about battling DeChambeau, Rahm was candid: "He’s been playing fantastic golf. When he gets going, he’s a very difficult man to beat." DeChambeau was equally respectful: "Jon Rahm, that guy — he’s too good. He’s one of the best in the world."

Augusta Awaits the Favourites

DeChambeau arrives with a career-best Masters trend: T6 in 2024, T5 in 2025. He has figured out the nuances of Augusta’s demands. Rahm brings something more — he’s a Masters champion. In 2023, he shot a closing 69 to win by four strokes, becoming the first European to claim both the U.S. Open and the Green Jacket. At Augusta, he knows not just the course, but the feeling of winning there.

The Wider Field

Ten LIV Golf players make up roughly 10 percent of the Masters field. Five are past champions, holding six green jackets between them: Garcia (2017), Johnson (2020), Watson (twice: 2012, 2014), Schwartzel (2011), and Rahm (2023). Sergio Garcia makes his 103rd major start. Cameron Smith, who has five top-10 finishes in his last eight Masters appearances, looks to bounce back after missing the cut last year. Tom McKibbin, the 23-year-old from Legion XIII, makes his debut after winning the Hong Kong Open.

But none of it matters much if DeChambeau or Rahm catch fire. They have the form, the talent, and now — with less than a week to go — they have the momentum.
